The Levites waited until the other tribes were taken care of before they presented their claim to Joshua. They based their claim on a very good foundation: not their own merits or services, but God’s command. The support of ministers is not something left merely to the will of the people, so that they may let them go without if they choose; those who preach the gospel should live by the gospel, and should live comfortably.

By mixing the Levites with the other tribes, they were made to see that all of Israel’s attention was on them, and therefore it was their concern to walk in a way so that their ministry would not be blamed. Every tribe had its share of cities for the Levites. Thus God graciously provided for keeping up religion among them, so that they might have the word in all parts of the land. Yet, blessed be God, we have the gospel more widely spread among us.

God promised to give to Abraham’s descendants the land of Canaan as a possession, and now they possessed it and lived there. And the promise of the heavenly Canaan is just as sure to all of God’s spiritual Israel; for it is the promise of Him who cannot lie. No one stood before them. The later presence of the Canaanites was the result of Israel's laziness and their sinful desire for the idolatries and abominations of the heathen whom they sheltered and indulged. Nothing failed of any good thing that the Lord had spoken to the house of Israel. In due season all his promises will be fulfilled; then his people will acknowledge that the Lord has exceeded their greatest expectations, and made them more than conquerors, and brought them to their desired rest.
